summary.intent: Install trufflehog as a pre-commit hook for the optimize-anything project to automatically scan for secrets before each commit.
summary.outcome: Successfully installed the pre-commit framework (v4.5.1) and configured trufflehog as a pre-commit hook with verified-only results and no-update flags, leaving all existing Entire CLI hooks intact.

repo: Project uses the 'Entire CLI' tool which installs its own git hooks (commit-msg, post-commit, pre-push, prepare-commit-msg) in .git/hooks/
Entire CLI had set core.hooksPath in .git/config pointing to .git/hooks/ (the default location), which was redundant but blocked pre-commit installation
Checkpoint files from Entire CLI contain UUID conversation identifiers that trufflehog flags as NPM tokens — confirmed benign false positives (observation #874)
trufflehog's auto-detect mode (when run under pre-commit) overrides explicit flags passed via args, requiring --trust-local-git-config to prevent this

code.finding: Final config uses: trufflehog git file://. --since-commit HEAD --results=verified --fail --trust-local-git-config --no-update. The language: system type uses the locally installed binary rather than downloading one.

workflow: trufflehog has three integration methods per PreCommit.md; 'repo: local' with 'language: system' is ideal for projects with trufflehog already installed via Homebrew
pre-commit framework blocks on core.hooksPath being set in git config even when it points to the default .git/hooks/ directory — must unset before running 'pre-commit install'
trufflehog --no-update flag is essential when running under pre-commit to suppress auto-updater errors that cause spurious non-zero exit codes
--results=verified filters to only confirmed secrets, avoiding noise from unverified/unknown detections like UUIDs misidentified as tokens

friction: pre-commit framework was not installed and had to be added via Homebrew before any configuration could proceed
core.hooksPath set by Entire CLI blocked pre-commit installation even though it pointed to the default directory
trufflehog auto-detect mode silently overrode explicit CLI flags when run under pre-commit, requiring --trust-local-git-config to enforce intended behavior
trufflehog auto-updater caused exit code 1 (failing the hook) even when 0 secrets were found, requiring --no-update flag
open_items: The .pre-commit-config.yaml should be committed to the repo so other contributors get the same hook setup

The two false positives (UUIDs in Entire checkpoint files flagged as NPM tokens) are suppressed by --results=verified but not addressed at the source — consider adding them to a trufflehog allowlist if verified-only mode ever needs to be relaxed
pre-commit was installed via Homebrew on this machine only; contributors will need to install it independently unless a setup script or README note is added
